summary: Iter 9 — Brain Reveal layer (P0+P1 additive). 16/16 backend pytest tests PASS. Frontend regression smoke: landing, /terms, /dashboard (unauth redirect), cookie consent all clean. Critically: data-testid='brain-reveal-strip' correctly does NOT render on unauthenticated landing, /terms, or unauth /dashboard — confirming `loading || !items.length` guard returns null cleanly. No console errors beyond expected 401s from auth-gated API calls after /dashboard redirect. Insight shape verified statically via insights_service._shape() — emits id, kind, headline, detail, source_signals(list), confidence(0-100), tone(neutral|positive|caution), suggested_action{label,route}, dismissible, created_at. MAX_INSIGHTS == 5. Orchestrator health still returns all 6 subscribers wired. Decision and legacy routes still mount and enforce 401 unauth.
critical_code_review_comments: insights_service._is_dismissed() projects {'_id':0,'_id_exists':1} which is invalid Mongo projection syntax — Mongo will reject mixing inclusion+exclusion (other than _id). The function is never called (replaced by _all_dismissed which is correct). Recommend deleting _is_dismissed() to avoid future foot-gun.

build_insights() loops `async for sc in mongo_db.salary_comparison.find(...)` and then appends without enforcing MAX_INSIGHTS inside the loop. With many sources, the function only caps via final slice insights[:5]. That's fine functionally but does extra Mongo round-trips on heavy users. Consider an early `if len(insights) >= MAX_INSIGHTS: break` after each append.

BrainReveal.jsx silently swallows non-401 errors with console.warn — good defensive posture for a passive surface. Dismiss is optimistic with no rollback on failure, which matches the spec (user already saw card disappear) but means a dismiss API outage results in card reappearing on next load — acceptable.

interview_prep_context insight uses ipc.get('from_addr','?') in the id; if from_addr is missing the id becomes 'interview_prep:?' which collides across recruiters. Low-risk because the orchestrator-side seeder always sets from_addr, but a guard is cheap.
Mongo index `insight_dismissals(user_id, insight_id)` unique is correctly added in server.py startup — verified.
Telemetry empty-dataset handling stays correct (no division by zero); confirmed in iter8 still passing.
test_credentials: Emergent OAuth — 401 path verified per request. No authenticated end-to-end run executed (matches main agent's prior manual seed-and-verify).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Brain Reveal layer additive surface is verified. Backend: GET /api/orchestrator/insights and POST /api/orchestrator/insights/{id}/dismiss both 401 unauth; shape emitter (_shape) and MAX_INSIGHTS=5 statically validated. Frontend: BrainReveal returns null cleanly when items empty and is correctly absent on landing/terms/unauth-dashboard. Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter9_brain_reveal.py are non-destructive (no auth required, only public-status assertions). Iter8 tests still relevant for orchestrator/health and decision routes.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no failures. One test was initially failing (decision/match expected /api/decision/match but actual route is POST /api/decision/match/{job_id}); test was corrected, not a product bug.
